settings.title: Settings
settings.about_desc: This app is dedicated to preserving and celebrating the rich history of Bengal through the stories of its heroes, from the Sultanate era to the 2024. App data collection is ongoing and community contributions are welcome.
settings.features_list:
• No ads - Pure educational focus
• Offline-first - Works without internet
• Bilingual - English & Bengali support

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.introduction_desc: At Bengal Heroes, we are committed to providing an educational experience about the history of Bengal. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle data within our application.
privacy.data_collection_desc: Bengal Heroes is an offline-first educational application. We do not collect personal information from our users. All historical data displayed in the app has been gathered from public internet sources, including but not limited to Banglapedia and historical archives.
privacy.accuracy_desc: While we strive for historical accuracy, we acknowledge that history can be complex and sometimes debated. If you find any factual errors or have concerns about the content provided, please reach out to us. We are committed to maintaining the integrity of the information presented.
privacy.contributions_desc: We welcome contributions from the community. If you have historical data, images, or stories that you believe should be included in Bengal Heroes, please contact us for possible inclusion in future updates.
privacy.contact_desc:
For corrections, data contributions, or any questions regarding this privacy policy, please contact us through our official channels. 
Contact:
Email: contact@bengalbytes.com
Phone: +880 1998470273

info.timeline_description: Bengal's rich history spans thousands of years, marked by the rise and fall of great empires, bloodless revolutions, and the struggle for independence. From the ancient kingdoms of the Maurya and Gupta empires to the Sultanate and Mughal periods, Bengal has been a center of power, culture, and intellectual thought. The colonial era brought new challenges, spurring a renaissance of Bengali culture and independence movements that ultimately led to the birth of modern India and Bangladesh. This timeline captures the pivotal moments that shaped Bengal and its people.
info.travelers_description: Throughout history, Bengal has captivated travelers and explorers from around the world. From ancient Chinese monks seeking Buddhist knowledge to European merchants and adventurers, Bengal was considered the golden jewel of Asia. These travelers left behind remarkable accounts of Bengal's wealth, culture, and people. Their writings provide invaluable insights into how the world perceived Bengal and its contributions to global trade, knowledge, and commerce. The travelers' accounts serve as a bridge between Bengal's glorious past and the world's recognition of its historical importance.
info.timeline_highlight_1: Multiple empires ruled Bengal, each leaving their mark on culture and architecture
info.timeline_highlight_2: The Bengal Renaissance sparked a revolutionary intellectual and cultural movement
info.timeline_highlight_3: Bengal played a crucial role in India's independence struggle
info.timeline_highlight_4: The partition of 1947 and Bangladesh's liberation in 1971 are pivotal events in Bengal's modern history
info.travelers_highlight_1: Famous travelers like Xuanzang and Faxian documented Bengal's prosperity
info.travelers_highlight_2: European merchants established trade routes bringing Bengal's goods to the world
info.travelers_highlight_3: Detailed accounts reveal Bengal's advanced civilization and culture
info.travelers_highlight_4: Travelers' journals provide authentic perspectives on Bengal's social and political systems
info.timeline_significance: Bengal's timeline is not just a chronicle of events, but a reflection of human endeavor, resilience, and progress. Each era contributed unique elements to Bengal's identity, from the philosophical contributions of ancient kingdoms to the scientific and literary renaissance of the 19th and 20th centuries. Understanding this timeline helps us appreciate the complexity and continuity of Bengal's civilization.
info.travelers_significance: The accounts of travelers provide us with a window into Bengal's past from an external perspective. These narratives challenge our understanding of history and validate Bengal's role as a major civilization. They remind us that Bengal was not isolated, but was an integral part of global exchange, influencing and being influenced by cultures across Asia and Europe.
